# ⚓ LearnDock

### Your On-Page Learning Dock
Note Capture • Focus Timer • Distraction Blocking • Auto-Categorisation

LearnDock is a Chrome Extension (Manifest V3) that injects a persistent sidebar into every page, automatically detects learning content, saves notes with full context, and enforces distraction blocking during timed focus sessions — all without leaving the page you're on.

---

## ✨ Features

### 📝 Note Capture — 5 Ways to Save
- **Popup note entry** — type and click **Save Note** from the extension popup
- **Add This Page** — saves the current page title and URL in one click
- **In-page sidebar** — type directly in the sidebar injected on every page, press Enter or click **Save**
- **Right-click highlight** — select any text, right-click, choose **Save to LearnDock**
- **Auto-capture** — on detected learning pages, LearnDock silently extracts the most meaningful sentence and saves it as an `auto-note`

Duplicate notes (same URL + same text) are automatically discarded.

---

### 🤖 Automatic Learning Page Detection

LearnDock uses an 11-signal scoring system to decide if a page is worth auto-capturing:

- Known learning domains (YouTube, Wikipedia, MDN, StackOverflow, Coursera, Udemy, GitHub, Medium, LeetCode, arXiv, and more)
- Learning keywords in title or URL (`tutorial`, `guide`, `crash course`, `documentation`, `explained`…)
- Structured headings, code blocks, math/equation elements
- Substantial body text (300+ words)
- `article`, `main`, or `[role="main"]` containers
- Table of contents presence
- Educational phrases (`you will learn`, `step 1`, `is defined as`…)
- Penalties for social feeds, login pages, and thin content

A page must reach a minimum score of **3** to trigger auto-capture.

---

### 🗂️ Automatic Note Categorisation

Every note is auto-assigned a category from the page title keywords. LearnDock strips stop words, matches against your existing categories, and either joins the closest match or creates a new category. Uncategorisable notes go to **General Learning**.

---

### 🗺️ Knowledge Map

Clicking ☰ in the popup opens the **Knowledge Map** — a full directory of all your note categories with counts. Click any category to jump to that group instantly.

---

### ⏱ Focus Timer (Pomodoro)

- Preset durations: **25m, 45m, 60m, 90m**
- Custom duration input (1–180 minutes)
- Live countdown with progress bar in the popup
- **Focus Mode** toggle activates site blocking across all tabs
- End-session confirmation dialog prevents accidental stops
- Desktop notification on natural completion
- Timer state persists across popup close/reopen via `chrome.storage.local`

---

### 🚫 Site Blocking

Blocks distracting sites during a focus session. Default blocked list:

`twitter.com` · `x.com` · `instagram.com` · `reddit.com` · `facebook.com` · `tiktok.com` · `netflix.com`

Fully customisable — add or remove domains via the popup. When a blocked site is visited, the full page is replaced with a lock screen showing the domain, session countdown, motivational quote, and exception button.

Also hides YouTube distractions (recommended videos, comments, Shorts, end cards) via CSS injection + MutationObserver.

---

### ⏳ Focus Exceptions

- Request **5 minutes** on a blocked site via the lock screen
- Maximum **4 exceptions per session**
- **60-minute cooldown** between exceptions
- Amber countdown banner shows remaining exception time on the page
- Exception count resets when a new session starts

---

### 🎨 In-Page Sidebar

An ⚓ anchor button is docked to the right edge of every page. The sidebar includes:

- Note input (Enter or Save)
- Full grouped notes library with expand/collapse
- Delete buttons per note (on hover)
- Video timestamp links — click to seek to that exact moment in the video
- **Dark / Light theme toggle** — preference saved to storage

---

### 🔔 Notifications

| Event | Notification |
|---|---|
| Manual or highlight note saved | "Saved to LearnDock" |
| Focus session completes | "Focus session complete! Great work 🎉" |
| Auto-notes | Silent (no notification) |

---

---

## 📦 Requirements

- Google Chrome (latest) or any Chromium-based browser
- No account or login required
- No internet connection required (all storage is local)

---

## 🚀 Installation

### 1️⃣ Download

1. Go to the repository page
2. Click **Code → Download ZIP**
3. Extract the ZIP to a folder on your computer

---

### 2️⃣ Load in Chrome

1. Open **Google Chrome**
2. Go to `chrome://extensions/`
3. Enable **Developer mode** (toggle, top-right)
4. Click **Load unpacked**
5. Select the extracted `LearnDock` folder
6. The ⚓ LearnDock icon will appear in your toolbar

---

## 🔄 Updating

1. Download the latest version from the repository
2. Extract the ZIP
3. Go to `chrome://extensions/`
4. Click **Remove** on the old version
5. Click **Load unpacked** and select the new folder

---

## 🛠 Usage

1. Click the ⚓ icon in the Chrome toolbar to open the popup
2. Select a focus duration and click **🎯 Start Focus Session**
3. Use **Save Note** or **+ Add This Page** to capture knowledge
4. Click the ⚓ button on the right edge of any page to open the sidebar
5. Browse your notes by category in the popup or sidebar

---

## 🐞 Troubleshooting

- Ensure **Developer mode** is enabled in `chrome://extensions/`
- Make sure you selected the extracted folder, not the ZIP file
- If the sidebar does not appear, try refreshing the page
- If blocked sites still load, confirm a focus session is active in the popup

---

## 🏗 Architecture

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Extension | Chrome Manifest V3 |
| UI Injection | Content script + DOM manipulation |
| Storage | `chrome.storage.local` (all local, no server) |
| Timer | `chrome.alarms` API + persisted end-timestamp |
| Background | Service worker (`background.js`) |
| Styling | Injected CSS with CSS variables (light + dark) |

---

## 📁 File Structure

```
LearnDock/
├── manifest.json       # Extension config (Manifest V3)
├── background.js       # Service worker: timer, alarms, context menu, storage
├── content.js          # Injected on all pages: sidebar, focus mode, auto-detection
├── popup.html          # Extension popup UI
├── popup.js            # Popup logic: timer, notes, blocked sites, navigation
├── popup.css           # Popup styles
├── sidebar.css         # Injected sidebar styles (light & dark mode)
├── icon16.png          # Toolbar icon (16×16)
├── icon48.png          # Toolbar icon (48×48)
└── icon128.png         # Toolbar icon (128×128)
```

---

## 🔐 Privacy & Data

- All data stored **locally** via `chrome.storage.local`
- Nothing is transmitted to any server
- No account, login, or email required
- Uninstalling the extension removes all stored data

---
